faults.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start, check engine light
faults.verdictImplication: Critical: Verify if TSB 21-2119 has been performed. If not, deduct $3,500 from valuation.

sources.name: NHTSA TSB 21-2119
sources.note: Official Ford TSB for 3.5L EcoBoost cam phasers

faults.symptoms: Harsh shifting, skipping gears
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: Software updates may fix it, but hardware failure requires rebuild.

faults.symptoms: Shuddering at highway speeds (The ''Chevy Shake'')
faults.verdictImplication: High Risk: If unchecked, destroys entire transmission ($5k).

sources.name: CarComplaints
sources.note: Widespread reports of torque converter shudder

faults.symptoms: Ticking noise, misfire, bent pushrods
faults.verdictImplication: Severe: Requires head removal. Avoid high mileage units without proof of repair.

faults.symptoms: Sagging rear end, compressor running constantly
faults.verdictImplication: Common: Plan for conversion to coil springs ($1,500) eventually.

faults.symptoms: Ticking noise on cold start (The 'Hemi Tick')
faults.verdictImplication: Annoyance: Often broken bolts. Inspect carefully before buying.

faults.symptoms: Shudder between 25-50 mph, check engine light
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: Software update might fix early stages, otherwise requires replacement.

faults.symptoms: Grinding noise on startup, failure to start
faults.verdictImplication: Minor: Common wear item but expensive on this model.

faults.symptoms: Low oil light, fouled spark plugs (V6 Only)
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: VCM muzzler device can prevent this if caught early.

faults.symptoms: Loss of power, whining noise, failure to accelerate
faults.verdictImplication: Critical: Avoid 2013-2015 models entirely. Transmission is a ticking time bomb.

faults.symptoms: Juttering, stalling, overheating
faults.verdictImplication: High Risk: Ensure fluid changes were done every 30k miles. Otherwise walk away.

faults.symptoms: Air conditioning blowing warm air
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: Extended warranty exists (10 years), verify VIN eligibility.

faults.symptoms: Gas smell in oil, rising oil level (1.5T engine)
faults.verdictImplication: Check: Verify software update 18-137 has been applied.

faults.symptoms: High pitched whine from transmission
faults.verdictImplication: Low Risk: Rare, but verify no abnormal noises during test drive.

faults.symptoms: Brief rattle/grind on cold start
faults.verdictImplication: Annoyance: Mostly noise, but can stretch chain over long term.

faults.symptoms: Severe vibration at idle
faults.verdictImplication: Minor: Technical Service Bulletin fixes exist but may not fully resolve.

faults.symptoms: Shudder at 20-50mph
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: Check for software update first.

faults.symptoms: Delayed engagement, high RPM before shifting
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: Often software related, but check for latest TSBs.

faults.symptoms: Perforation on frame rails
faults.verdictImplication: Critical: Inspect frame coating thoroughly before purchase.

faults.symptoms: Coolant leak, overheating
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: Inspect for pink crust around pulley.

faults.symptoms: Violent steering wheel shaking after hitting bump
faults.verdictImplication: Common: Usually track bar or ball joints. Negotiate repair cost.

faults.symptoms: Electrical ghosts, fuel pump relay failure
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: Can cause stalling.

faults.symptoms: Vehicle sagging, error messages
faults.verdictImplication: High Risk: Avoid air suspension on used models if possible.

faults.symptoms: White smoke, misfire, coolant loss (1.5L/1.6L)
faults.verdictImplication: Critical: Requires engine replacement. Avoid 1.5L/1.6L engines.

faults.symptoms: Slipping, hard shifts
faults.verdictImplication: Severe: 6F35 transmission is weak point.

faults.symptoms: Coolant in oil (Chocolate milk oil)
faults.verdictImplication: Severe: Internal water pump failure destroys engine.

faults.symptoms: Propane smell, leaks, grinding
faults.verdictImplication: Common: Power Transfer Unit needs frequent fluid changes.

faults.symptoms: Low oil light, engine noise (early models)
faults.verdictImplication: Severe: Can lead to timing chain failure.

faults.symptoms: No A/C, leaking refrigerant
faults.verdictImplication: Common: Poor weld quality on factory condensers.

faults.symptoms: Vibration at cruise speeds
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: Fluid flush with specific additive often fixes it.

faults.symptoms: Doors won't lock/unlock electronically
faults.verdictImplication: Annoyance: Expensive if all fail.

faults.symptoms: Shudder or vibration at low speeds
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: Software update or fluid change may help.

faults.symptoms: Flickering or failure
faults.verdictImplication: Minor: Recall issued for some years.

faults.symptoms: Jerking or hesitation when accelerating
faults.verdictImplication: Critical: Extended warranty to 100k miles. Verify coverage.

faults.symptoms: Low oil light, engine noise
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: Short block replacement often needed.

faults.symptoms: Ticking/knocking noise from engine
faults.verdictImplication: Severe: Precursor to engine failure (Nu 2.0L).

faults.symptoms: Delay when accelerating from stop (1.6T)
faults.verdictImplication: Common: Characteristic of the DCT, software updates help.

faults.symptoms: Knocking, seizing (Theta II)
faults.verdictImplication: Critical: Verify if engine has been replaced under recall.

faults.symptoms: Rod knock, metallic noise
faults.verdictImplication: Critical: Oil starvation issue. Check for recall completion.

faults.symptoms: Overheating, coolant loss
faults.verdictImplication: Severe: 3.3L V6 head bolts can strip.

faults.symptoms: Infotainment selects items unexpectedly
faults.verdictImplication: Annoyance: Screen replacement usually required.

faults.symptoms: Misfire, rough idle
faults.verdictImplication: Minor: Common maintenance item.

faults.symptoms: Rattle at startup, jump timing
faults.verdictImplication: Critical: Engine interference design. Failure destroys engine.

faults.symptoms: RPM fluctuations, failure to move
faults.verdictImplication: Critical: High failure rate. Avoid used units without warranty.

faults.symptoms: Shuddering during acceleration
faults.verdictImplication: Severe: Known 2013-2014 issue ('Judder Code P17F0').

faults.symptoms: Error message when car is in park
faults.verdictImplication: Minor: Switch in shifter assembly fails.

faults.symptoms: Rattle from engine/transmission mating area
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: Requires transmission removal to replace.

faults.symptoms: Rough shifting, delayed engagement
faults.verdictImplication: High Risk: Early models (2014-2015) particularly bad.

faults.symptoms: Interior panels warping in heat
faults.verdictImplication: Cosmetic: Glue failure.

faults.symptoms: Water accumulation in tail light
faults.verdictImplication: Cosmetic: Expensive assembly to replace.

faults.symptoms: Humming noise increases with speed
faults.verdictImplication: Minor: Rear bearings fail prematurely.

faults.symptoms: Squeaking/creaking suspension
faults.verdictImplication: Common: Upper control arm ball joints dry out.

faults.symptoms: No heat in winter
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: 2020-2021 models had sensor failures.

faults.symptoms: Stalling, failure to restart
faults.verdictImplication: Recall: Denso fuel pump recall. Verify completion.

faults.symptoms: Whining noise (N20 engine)
faults.verdictImplication: Critical: Plastic guides break. 2012-2015 models most at risk.

faults.symptoms: Misfire, loss of compression (C300)
faults.verdictImplication: Critical: Wrist pin alignment issue on early W205s.

faults.symptoms: Coolant leak
faults.verdictImplication: Common: Thermo-management module leaks.

faults.symptoms: Touch screen spiderwebbing or unresponsive
faults.verdictImplication: Common: Delamination of touch layer.

faults.symptoms: Rattling on startup (3.5L EcoBoost)
faults.verdictImplication: Severe: Same issue as F-150.

faults.symptoms: Engine knocking (V6)
faults.verdictImplication: Critical: Recall exists, verify engine health.

faults.symptoms: Freon leak, dashboard removal needed
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: Labor intensive repair.

faults.symptoms: Truck dies instantly, 'Low Fuel Pressure', no restart
faults.verdictImplication: CATASTROPHIC: Pump sends metal shavings into all injectors and lines. Total fuel system replacement required.

faults.symptoms: Violent shaking of steering wheel after irregular road surface
faults.verdictImplication: Severe: Track bar and damper failure. Dangerous at highway speeds.

faults.symptoms: Slipping gears, burnt fluid smell, limp mode
faults.verdictImplication: Critical: The stock transmission cannot handle the Cummins torque. Expect failure near 150k miles.

faults.symptoms: Bolt falls into intake manifold
faults.verdictImplication: Engine Killer: A $2 bolt can destroy a $12k engine. Inspect immediately.

faults.symptoms: Rattling noise from engine front (5.0L SC)
faults.verdictImplication: Financial Ruin: Requires engine removal. Often exceeds vehicle value. SELL IMMEDIATELY.

faults.symptoms: Vehicle sagging, 'Suspension Fault' error
faults.verdictImplication: Common: Compressor wears out due to leak overwork.

faults.symptoms: Blue smoke from exhaust after idling (N63 V8)
faults.verdictImplication: High Risk: Engine burns extensive oil. Repair labor is intensive.

faults.symptoms: High pitched whine (N20/N26 4-cyl models)
faults.verdictImplication: Critical: Plastic guides shatter and clog oil pickup.

faults.symptoms: Chirping noise during engagement (3.0T)
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: Wear item on high mileage units.

faults.symptoms: Low oil light every 500 miles
faults.verdictImplication: Severe: Piston ring issues on early 3.0T/2.0T engines. Requires rebuild.

faults.symptoms: Door stuck, motor grinding, battery drain
faults.verdictImplication: Common: Cable or motor failure. Expensive convenience repair.

faults.symptoms: Rough shifting, hunting for gears
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: Software updates help, but torque converter may fail.

faults.symptoms: Misfire, coolant consumption (Hybrid Models)
faults.verdictImplication: Critical: Widespread issue on Hybrid Pacificas. Engine replacement likely.

faults.symptoms: Engine cuts out at speed (PHEV)
faults.verdictImplication: Dangerous: Transmission wiring harness recall. Verify VIN.

faults.symptoms: Ticking noise, misfire, 'Service Stabilitrak'
faults.verdictImplication: Severe: High failure rate on early L83 engines. Requires partial engine teardown.

faults.symptoms: Door won't open or close automatically
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: Common convenience failure. Cable and motor assembly replacement.

faults.symptoms: Whining, juddering, loss of power
faults.verdictImplication: Critical: Cost often exceeds 50% of vehicle market value. SELL RECOMMENDED.

faults.symptoms: Vehicle sagging, bumpy ride, compressor running constantly
faults.verdictImplication: High Risk: Expensive Nitro-charged system. Consider coil spring conversion.

faults.symptoms: Rattle on start, engine won't turn over (Valve jump)
faults.verdictImplication: Severe: Can result in complete engine destruction if it skips a tooth.
Moderate: A known weak point in the B48/B58 engines. Requires replacement with updated unit.
faults.symptoms: Oil leak onto hot engine, burning smell, coolant contamination

faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: Extremely common on VAG 2.0T engines. Plastic housing warps.
faults.symptoms: Overheating warning, puddles under front of car

faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: Mercedes multi-link suspension is sensitive to bushing wear.
faults.symptoms: Clunking over bumps, uneven tire wear

faults.verdictImplication: Severe: Critical failure on early Drive-E engines. Requires engine rebuild or replacement.
faults.symptoms: Low oil light every 1000 miles, blue smoke

faults.verdictImplication: Severe: High risk of catastrophic engine failure. Check recall status immediately.
faults.symptoms: Knocking noise, oil light flicker, engine stall

faults.verdictImplication: Severe: Major reliability hazard. Repair cost often exceeds 50% of value on high-mileage units.
faults.symptoms: Metallic rattling, sudden loss of power

faults.verdictImplication: CATASTROPHIC: Coolant leaks into cylinders due to block design. Engine replacement required.
faults.symptoms: White smoke, misfires on startup, chronic low coolant

faults.verdictImplication: Severe: High labor cost for a gasket repair. Often deferred by owners.
faults.symptoms: Oil seeping onto exhaust manifold, burning smell

faults.verdictImplication: Critical: Radiator failure mixes coolant into transmission. Destroys the gearbox. Pre-2010 models only.
faults.symptoms: Transmission slipping, pink frothy fluid

faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: Electrical/Control issue common on Hydra-Matic 9T65.
faults.symptoms: Erratic shifting, won't move from Park, limp mode

faults.verdictImplication: Severe: Lifter roller fails and eats the camshaft. Requires major engine teardown.
faults.symptoms: Rhythmic ticking noise at idle, misfires

faults.verdictImplication: Severe: TigerShark 2.4L engine flaw. May require engine replacement if out of warranty.
faults.symptoms: Engine stalling on turns due to low oil, no warning light
Oil seepage near transfer case, judder under load, driveline vibration on acceleration
faults.verdictImplication: Severe: Porsche issued a U.S. technical bulletin because oil leakage at the transfer box can require full transfer-box replacement, not just a seal. On a used Macan, this is a real fix-or-sell bill.

sources.name: Porsche TSB MC-10249866-0001
sources.note: Porsche technical information for Macan (95B) transfer box oil leakage and replacement criteria.

faults.symptoms: Coolant temperature too high warning, coolant loss, overheating risk under load
faults.verdictImplication: High cost: Porsche instructs technicians to inspect both the coolant pump and change-over valve because the root cause can cascade. If overheating is already showing up, this is not a cheap hose fix.

sources.name: Porsche TSB MC-11005795-0001
sources.note: Porsche technical information covering coolant pump and change-over valve faults on Macan S/GTS/Turbo.

faults.symptoms: Humming or whining from rear axle at highway speed, vibration on throttle tip-in and coast-down
faults.verdictImplication: Severe: Genesis has a U.S. differential inspection-and-repair bulletin because some GV70s need lock-nut correction while others need a full differential. Buyers should treat this as a major driveline issue, not tire noise.

sources.name: Genesis TSB MC-11000781-0001
sources.note: Genesis U.S. bulletin for rear differential hum or whine inspection and repair on GV70.

faults.symptoms: No warning in normal use; unresolved recall shows up in VIN check and materially affects saleability
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: Recall 012G is free when open, but an unresolved pretensioner VIN on a used GV70 is still a trust and transaction hit. If recall status is unknown, treat it as a must-verify issue before buying or selling.

sources.name: Genesis Recall 012G
sources.note: Genesis U.S. recall bulletin for GV70 front seat belt pretensioner protection cap installation.

faults.symptoms: Shudder or shake at low speed, driveline vibration on light throttle, repeated fluid exchange with no lasting fix
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate to Severe: GM's own bulletin says contaminated transmission fluid and torque-converter damage can sit behind low-speed shudder complaints. On a used U.S. Blazer, this is one of the clearest fix-or-walk transmission signals.

sources.name: GM Bulletin 23-NA-072
sources.note: Official GM bulletin for 2019-2025 Chevrolet Blazer low-speed shudder tied to TCC shudder and possible torque-converter damage.

faults.symptoms: Check-engine light, rough running, misfire-like behavior, injector diagnosis that does not solve the issue
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: GM says some Blazers flagged for fuel-balance faults actually have damaged switching roller finger followers. This is not a small tune-up; it pushes the car into real valvetrain labor.

sources.name: GM Bulletin 22-NA-193
sources.note: Official GM bulletin covering Blazer LGX valvetrain damage behind P219A/P219B fuel-balance codes.

faults.symptoms: Check-engine light, harsh shifting, no-signal transmission speed sensor faults, limp-mode behavior
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: Genesis published a dedicated G70 bulletin for E-module replacement when transmission fluid temperature and speed sensor faults are present. It is a real repair event, not normal maintenance.

sources.name: Genesis TSB 19-AT-001-G
sources.note: Official Genesis bulletin for G70 E-module replacement tied to transmission fluid temperature and input/output speed sensor DTCs.

faults.component: Left Turbo Oil Feed Pipe Leak / Fire Risk (3.3T)
faults.symptoms: Oil smell, smoke from engine bay, visible oil leakage near turbo area, fire risk if ignored
faults.verdictImplication: Severe: On 3.3T cars this is a real stop-driving issue. Even if recall coverage applies, a used-car buyer should treat unresolved turbo oil-line leakage as a major trust and safety defect.

sources.name: Genesis Recall 021G / NHTSA 24V169
sources.note: Official Genesis recall for the left turbocharger oil feed pipe on 2019-2022 G70 3.3T vehicles.

faults.symptoms: Low-speed shudder during light throttle, shake on gear changes, repeat transmission fluid service with recurring symptom
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: The second-gen Enclave uses GM's 9-speed family and GM has separate Enclave bulletins for TCC shudder across 2018 and 2019-2024. On a used U.S. Enclave, unresolved shudder is a meaningful ownership-cost signal.

sources.name: GM Bulletin 18-NA-091
sources.note: Official GM bulletin for 2018 Enclave slight shudder during slow-speed gear shifts caused by TCC shudder.

sources.name: GM Bulletin 23-NA-072
sources.note: Official GM bulletin extending low-speed shudder / torque converter diagnostics to 2019-2024 Buick Enclave.

faults.symptoms: Grinding noise, reduced motive power, vehicle movement in Park without EPB, difficulty engaging Drive or Reverse
faults.verdictImplication: Severe: Kia's U.S. recall says partial engagement can damage shaft splines and produce grinding, loss of drive, or unintended movement in Park. If this symptom is present, treat it as a serious driveline event.

sources.name: Kia Recall 24V214
sources.note: Kia U.S. recall for Telluride intermediate shaft and right front driveshaft partial engagement.

faults.symptoms: Rear suspension sag, oil leakage at rear shocks, floaty ride or instability over bumps
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: The U.S. TSB confirms rear self-leveling shock replacement on Telluride. This is not catastrophic, but it is a real four-figure ownership hit on a family SUV that buyers often assume is trouble-free.

sources.name: Kia TSB CHA125 Rev 1
sources.note: Kia U.S. bulletin for self-leveling rear shock absorber inspection and replacement on Telluride.

faults.symptoms: Abnormal steering feel, front suspension noise, structural cracking around the strut mount area
faults.verdictImplication: Severe: Lexus recall 22LA02 involves potentially missing spot-welds at the front shock absorber mounting area. If unresolved, this is a structural safety defect, not a cosmetic campaign.

sources.name: Lexus Recall 22LA02
sources.note: Lexus U.S. recall for potentially missing spot-welds near the front shock absorber mounting area on 2022 NX.

faults.component: Gear Shift Control Unit Logic Fault
faults.symptoms: Unexpected shift behavior, warning messages, drivability complaints resolved by ECU update
faults.verdictImplication: Low to Moderate: Lexus issued a U.S. special service campaign for gear shift control software. It is usually a software fix, but unresolved campaign status still hurts confidence on an otherwise premium compact SUV.

sources.name: Lexus Campaign 22LC03
sources.note: Lexus U.S. special service campaign for gear shift control unit software on 2022 NX 250/350.

faults.symptoms: Seat belt tongue may not latch correctly or may unexpectedly unlatch while occupied
faults.verdictImplication: Low to Moderate: This is a safety-critical concern, but it is usually handled as a recall or investigation item rather than a large mechanical bill. For a used U.S. Palisade, unresolved seat-belt status is a trust and transaction hit more than a money-pit event.

sources.name: NHTSA PE24021
sources.note: ODI investigation into inadvertent seat belt unlatching on 2020-2023 Hyundai Palisade.

sources.name: Hyundai Recall 283 / NHTSA 25V607
sources.note: Recall filing for out-of-spec seat belt buckle components on 2020-2025 Palisade.

faults.component: Tow Hitch Harness Short / Fire Risk (If Equipped)
faults.symptoms: Burning smell near rear bumper, tow harness fault, smoke or fire risk near hitch wiring
faults.verdictImplication: Conditional Risk: This only applies to Palisades equipped with the Hyundai accessory tow hitch harness. If equipped and unresolved, treat it as a must-fix fire-risk issue before sale or continued ownership.

sources.name: Hyundai Recall 235 / NHTSA 22V633
sources.note: U.S. recall acknowledgement for Palisade tow hitch harness short/fire risk.

faults.symptoms: Strong clutch odor, clutch slippage, warning messages, possible bellhousing damage
faults.verdictImplication: Severe: This is a real fix-or-sell issue on manual Gladiators. Stellantis states the clutch pressure plate can overheat and fracture, and the repair is not a small consumable-service bill.

sources.name: Stellantis Recall 19A / NHTSA 23V116
sources.note: Official recall remedy for manual transmission clutch pressure plate overheating and fracture on Gladiator.

faults.symptoms: Wandering at highway speed, excessive steering play, lead or pull left/right
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: The official TSB calls for steering gear replacement and software updates. It usually will not total the truck, but it materially changes ownership confidence on a U.S. road-trip vehicle.

sources.name: Jeep TSB 08-003-21
sources.note: Technical bulletin for improved steering feel involving steering gear replacement on 2020 Gladiator.

faults.component: Low-Pressure Fuel Pump Fuse / Stall Risk
faults.symptoms: No-start, sudden stall, fuel system warnings, blown low-pressure fuel pump fuse
faults.verdictImplication: Low to Moderate: Volvo treated this as a safety recall, so the direct repair is often cheap or free. For a used U.S. XC60, the bigger issue is making sure the recall was completed and the car is not carrying unresolved fuel-system stall risk.

sources.name: Volvo Recall R10100 / NHTSA 21V414
sources.note: Official recall acknowledgement for low-pressure fuel pump fuse failure on 2019-2020 XC60.

faults.symptoms: Rear glass suddenly shatters, popping sound from rear hatch area, broken defroster grid window
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: This is not a drivetrain money pit, but it is a real ownership cost on late-model RDXs and can become a transaction issue if it has not been corrected properly.

sources.name: Acura Bulletin A24-012
sources.note: Acura service bulletin for inspection and replacement of shattered rear windshield on 2019-2024 RDX.

faults.symptoms: High-pitched front or rear brake squeal below 15 mph, especially when cold
faults.verdictImplication: Low Risk: This is a nuisance-cost item rather than a platform-defining fault. The main value of recording it is to avoid overstating the RDX as a money-pit when U.S. service data points more toward smaller quality issues than catastrophic repairs.

sources.name: Acura Service Bulletin 19-053
sources.note: Official Acura bulletin covering front/rear brake squeal when lightly applied at low speeds on 2019-2024 RDX.

faults.symptoms: Strong fuel smell, smoke under hood, rough running, fuel pressure drop
faults.verdictImplication: Severe: Ford's recall says a cracked injector can leak fuel near ignition sources. Even if the formal remedy is a software-and-drain-tube campaign, an affected used Bronco Sport should be treated as a real underhood-fire risk until resolved.

sources.name: Ford Recall 22S73 / NHTSA 22V859
sources.note: Ford safety recall notice for cracked fuel injectors on 2021-2023 Bronco Sport 1.5L.

sources.name: Ford Recall 25S76 / NHTSA 25V467
sources.note: Expanded Ford recall population for cracked fuel injectors on 2021-2024 Bronco Sport.

faults.symptoms: Sudden battery degradation, stall at low speed after auto stop/start, loss of electrical accessories
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: Usually not a giant out-of-pocket bill, but a sudden 12-volt failure can strand the driver and wreck confidence in the vehicle. On a used U.S. Bronco Sport, open recall status matters.

sources.name: Ford Recall 25S02 / NHTSA 25V019
sources.note: Ford owner notice for sudden 12-volt battery degradation on 2021-2024 Bronco Sport.

faults.verdictImplication: Very Low Risk: Highly reliable platform. Repairs are typically only wear items.
faults.symptoms: N/A
Low coolant warning, coolant smell from rear, visible seepage near pump housing
faults.verdictImplication: High cost: Early 992 cars have a documented coolant-pump leak path. If coolant traces are present, budget for pump and seal replacement instead of repeated top-offs.

sources.name: Porsche TSB MC-11008155-0001
sources.note: Porsche technical information for coolant pump leakage on 2020 911 (992).

faults.symptoms: Engine mount warning, vibration at idle, drivetrain movement under load
faults.verdictImplication: Severe: Failed active mounts are expensive and usually replaced in pairs. A vibration complaint on a 992 is not a cheap rubber-mount job.

sources.name: Porsche TSB MC-10171900-0001
sources.note: Porsche technical information covering defective dynamic engine mounts on 911 (992).

faults.symptoms: Oil drips on undertray, underbody oil film, burn smell after spirited driving
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate to severe: Oil leaks on a rear-engine Porsche are labor heavy. If the belly pan is wet, the bill is usually far above a simple gasket reseal.

sources.name: Porsche TSB MC-11001406-0001
sources.note: Porsche technical information for oil leaks from the oil pan return seal on 911 (992).

faults.symptoms: Gear oil seepage around transfer case housing, vibration or judder under load
faults.verdictImplication: Severe: Once the transfer box starts leaking, the fix is usually seal-intensive and labor heavy. Ignore it and AWD drivability can deteriorate fast.

sources.name: Porsche TSB MC-11009165-0001
sources.note: Porsche technical information for oil leakage on the transfer box housing for Cayenne E3.

faults.symptoms: Coolant loss, sweet smell after shutdown, cooling system pressure loss
faults.verdictImplication: High cost: Cooling leaks on the E3 often turn into multiple-part jobs once the intake-side plumbing is opened. Budget more than a basic hose replacement.

sources.name: Porsche TSB MC-11015524-0001
sources.note: Porsche technical information covering leaks at the coolant change-over valve on Cayenne E3.

faults.symptoms: Loss of boost, hissing under acceleration, reduced power
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: A leaking charge-air hose is cheaper than transfer case work, but on a turbo Cayenne it still produces a real drivability bill and should not be dismissed as 'just software.'

sources.name: Porsche TSB MC-11009273-0001
sources.note: Porsche technical information for leaking charge-air pressure hose on Cayenne E3.

faults.symptoms: Humming or whining from the rear at 25-50 mph, vibration on accel/decel
faults.verdictImplication: Severe: Genesis has a formal inspection-and-repair bulletin because some units need retorque, others need a full differential assembly. If the noise is present, do not assume it is tire roar.

sources.name: Genesis TSB MC-11000783-0001
sources.note: Genesis rear differential hum or whine inspection and repair bulletin for GV80.

faults.symptoms: Oil seepage near turbo plumbing, burnt oil smell, drip marks on undertray
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: Oil-feed leaks are rarely catastrophic on day one, but labor is enough that a 'small leak' still matters in a fix-or-sell decision.

sources.name: Genesis TSB MC-11010484-0001
sources.note: Genesis service bulletin for oil leakage from the turbocharger oil feed pipe on GV80.

faults.symptoms: Weak cooling, refrigerant loss, oily residue at HVAC line joints
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: HVAC leaks are not drivetrain failures, but they create a real ownership bill and usually show up before buyers expect a luxury SUV to need air-conditioning work.

sources.name: Genesis TSB MC-11009327-0001
sources.note: Genesis service bulletin for refrigerant leakage at the evaporator pipe O-ring on GV80.

faults.symptoms: Oil leakage around transfer box, judder under load, AWD driveline vibration
faults.verdictImplication: Severe: Porsche has issued transfer box technical information and workshop campaigns because leak-related failures can require full transfer box replacement. On a used Macan, this is not a cosmetic leak.

sources.name: Porsche TSB MC-10249866-0001
sources.note: Porsche technical information describing oil leakage at the transfer box on Macan (95B).

sources.name: Porsche WPR1 Workshop Campaign
sources.note: Porsche workshop campaign to replace affected Macan transfer boxes due to oil leakage risk.

faults.symptoms: Humming or whining from rear axle, vibration at speed, driveline resonance on throttle lift
faults.verdictImplication: Severe: Genesis has a formal inspection and repair bulletin because some GV70s need lock-nut retorque while others need a full differential or driveline component replacement.

sources.name: Genesis TSB MC-11009768-0001
sources.note: Official Genesis bulletin for GV70 rear differential hum or whine inspection and repair.

faults.symptoms: Grinding or binding at low speeds, driveline shudder on turns, front-to-rear power transfer hesitation
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate to Severe: US owner forums report transfer case replacements on early GV70s after driveline noise complaints. If a GV70 already has this symptom set, the repair is too large to ignore in a buy-or-fix decision.

sources.name: Genesis Owners - GV70 potential buyer concerned about transfer case
sources.note: US owner thread describing dealer familiarity with differential and transfer case replacements on GV70.

faults.symptoms: Most US complaints center on 12V battery drain, software glitches, or roof noise rather than a consistent high-cost drivetrain failure
faults.verdictImplication: Low Risk: Current US-market evidence does not show a strong money-pit mechanical pattern on the 2022+ NX. Treat it as a generally safe platform, but check for battery-drain complaints and outstanding recall work.

sources.name: ClubLexus NX 12V battery drain thread
sources.note: US owner reports point to parasitic 12V battery drain rather than an expensive systemic powertrain defect.

sources.name: 2022 Lexus NX shock-mount recall coverage
sources.note: Safety recall exists, but the main early ownership signal is recall/warranty activity rather than a repeat out-of-pocket failure.

faults.symptoms: Clunking or harsh rear suspension, sagging rear ride height, repeated rear shock replacement
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate: This is not usually a total-loss failure, but US owners repeatedly report expensive rear shock replacements outside warranty, especially on tow-package and self-leveling setups.

sources.name: Kia Telluride Forums - Rear self-leveling shock absorbers have failed
sources.note: US owners report failures from roughly 25k-70k miles and dealer quotes near $2k.

sources.name: Kia Telluride Forums - Self leveling shocks out again
sources.note: Repeat failure reports with out-of-warranty repair quotes around $2,500.

faults.symptoms: Grinding noise, reduced drive power, binding or driveline shock in AWD operation
faults.verdictImplication: Moderate to Severe: Kia has a formal US recall for intermediate shaft engagement problems, and owner reports suggest some AWD driveline repairs go beyond a simple software update.

sources.name: Kia Recall 24V214 / SC303
sources.note: Official US recall describing partial engagement damage of the intermediate shaft and right front driveshaft.

sources.name: Kia Telluride Forums - Multiple AWD / 4WD Coupler Failures
sources.note: Owner reports suggest repeated coupler-related driveline failures beyond a single isolated event.
